old_claim.note: Municipal Water Treatment provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Early municipal drinking-water treatment is anchored by the 1804 Paisley filter and later slow-sand municipal systems.

source_shape.source_locator: Historical review of water treatment through the ages, including early municipal filtration chronology.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The review places early municipal water-treatment practice in the lineage of slow sand filtration rather than making municipal treatment a prerequisite for slow sand filtration.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports slow sand filtration as the historical method feeding broad municipal water treatment; it does not support the reversed claim that the broader municipal category had to exist first.

ontology_before: The graph made the component/process node depend on the broader municipal treatment category, reversing component-to-system lineage.
ontology_after: Broad municipal water treatment now points back to slow sand filtration as a historical predecessor.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-10-random-sample-quality-fixes keeps the reversed edge absent and the corrected edge typed as historical_predecessor.
would_reject_if: The municipal_water_treatment node were narrowed to a later administrative utility category that postdates slow sand filtration.
A source showed slow sand filtration was invented only inside an already established municipal treatment system.
A future split moved early municipal filtration into a more precise node and removed the broad municipal edge.
short_reason: Slow sand filtration is upstream of broad municipal water treatment, not downstream.
